reasoning: The document's functional utility is severely compromised because the color-coded legend, which is essential for interpreting the Workforce Development Board map, has been stripped of its visual swatches and layout. While programmatic text recall is high (0.879), the failure to preserve the relationship between the colors and the labels constitutes a major visual and structural defect (Visual Score: 0.5). Following the principle that a bad document auto-approved is 3x more costly, this requires human intervention to restore the legend's functionality.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF features a color-coded legend organized in a 4-column grid that allows users to map colors on the North Carolina map to specific Workforce Development Boards. The HTML output collapses this into a single-column bulleted list and removes the color swatches entirely. concerns.source_pdf_quote: A 4-column grid legend at the bottom of the page where each name (e.g., 'Cape Fear', 'Durham') is preceded by a color-coded box corresponding to regions on the map.
concerns.output_quote: A single-column bulleted list of names ('• Cape Fear', '• Capital Area', etc.) with no color swatches or indicators linking them back to the map colors.
concerns.location: Legend section, following the map image.

concerns.analysis: Meaningful images/icons are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the 20 color-coded swatches in the legend, which are essential for interpreting the document's content (mapping colors to board names), have been omitted. Additionally, the 'NC works' logo located in the bottom right corner of the source PDF is missing from the rendered HTML screenshot.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 20 color-coded square swatches in the legend area; 'NC works' logo in the bottom right corner of the page.
concerns.output_quote: The legend list consists of plain text bullets only; there is no 'NC works' logo at the bottom of the page or in any other location.
concerns.location: Legend area and footer area of the document.

action_items: Restore the color swatches or provide an accessible text-based method to map the legend items to the map regions
Reformat the legend into a structured grid or table to match the source document's organization
Ensure the NC Works logo is included with appropriate alternative text
